mundo.titulo: G7 reaches agreement on global AI governance framework
mundo.resumen: Leaders of the Group of Seven nations have agreed to a landmark framework for regulating artificial intelligence, establishing common standards for high-risk AI systems and requiring transparency in AI-generated content. The deal, reached after months of negotiations, sets a new international benchmark for AI oversight.

mundo.titulo: Ukraine-Russia talks resume in Istanbul with US mediation
mundo.resumen: Diplomatic negotiations between Ukraine and Russia have resumed in Istanbul under US and Turkish mediation, marking the most significant direct talks in over two years. Officials report both sides have presented preliminary ceasefire proposals, though major obstacles remain around territorial issues and security guarantees.

mundo.titulo: WHO declares end of mpox emergency as global cases decline
mundo.resumen: The World Health Organization has officially declared an end to the global mpox public health emergency, citing a significant decline in reported cases worldwide. Health officials attributed the success to vaccination campaigns and improved surveillance systems developed since the 2022 outbreak.

mundo.titulo: India surpasses Japan as world's third-largest economy by GDP
mundo.resumen: India has officially overtaken Japan to become the world's third-largest economy in nominal GDP terms, according to IMF data released this week. The milestone reflects India's sustained growth trajectory of over 7% annually, driven by manufacturing expansion, digital services exports, and a young demographic dividend.

mundo.titulo: China's export surge intensifies trade tensions ahead of WTO summit
mundo.resumen: China's manufacturing exports have surged to record highs in Q1 2026, triggering new trade tensions with the EU and US ahead of a critical WTO summit. Both Western blocs are considering coordinated tariff measures on Chinese EVs, solar panels, and semiconductors, raising concerns about a potential new trade war.

mundo.titulo: Federal Reserve signals two more rate cuts expected in 2026
mundo.resumen: Fed Chair Jerome Powell indicated at a Senate hearing that the Federal Reserve expects to cut interest rates twice more before year-end, contingent on continued progress in bringing inflation to the 2% target. Current inflation stands at 2.3%, down from a peak of 9.1% in mid-2022, as the central bank navigates a soft landing.

negocios.titulo: OpenAI reaches $300B valuation after record $5B funding round
negocios.resumen: OpenAI has closed a $5 billion funding round, valuing the company at $300 billion and making it one of the most valuable private companies in history. The round was led by SoftBank and Microsoft, with significant participation from sovereign wealth funds. Proceeds will fund next-generation model training and global data center expansion.

negocios.titulo: Nvidia Q1 earnings beat expectations with $45B revenue milestone
negocios.resumen: Nvidia reported $45 billion in quarterly revenue for Q1 2026, surpassing analyst estimates by 12% and marking a new milestone in the company's AI-driven growth story. Data center revenue accounted for $38 billion, driven by continued demand for H200 and upcoming Blackwell Ultra GPU chips from hyperscalers and enterprise AI deployments.

negocios.titulo: Apple acquires AI startup Perplexity in $8B deal
negocios.resumen: Apple has acquired AI search startup Perplexity in an $8 billion deal, its largest acquisition to date. The move signals Apple's intention to fundamentally rebuild Siri using conversational AI search technology, directly competing with Google and Microsoft Copilot for AI assistant dominance across mobile and desktop platforms.

negocios.titulo: European Central Bank cuts rates to 2% as eurozone economy stabilizes
negocios.resumen: The European Central Bank reduced its key interest rate to 2.0%, the lowest level since 2022, as eurozone inflation fell to 1.9% and economic growth showed signs of stabilization. ECB President Christine Lagarde emphasized a data-dependent approach for future decisions amid continued uncertainty from global trade tensions.

negocios.titulo: Global EV sales hit 40% of all new cars in April 2026
negocios.resumen: Electric vehicles accounted for 40% of all new car sales globally in April 2026, according to the International Energy Agency, a milestone reached three years ahead of earlier forecasts. China continues to dominate with 65% EV penetration, while Europe reached 38% and the US lagged at 25%, driven by lower-cost Chinese and domestic models.

negocios.titulo: Amazon Web Services launches sovereign cloud regions across Latin America
negocios.resumen: Amazon Web Services announced the opening of sovereign cloud regions in Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia, designed to comply with local data residency laws and serve the booming Latin American digital economy. The $4 billion infrastructure investment signals growing enterprise cloud adoption across the region.

tecnologia.titulo: Google DeepMind releases Gemini Ultra 3 with scientific reasoning breakthrough
tecnologia.resumen: Google DeepMind unveiled Gemini Ultra 3, claiming landmark performance on scientific reasoning benchmarks including a first AI score above 90% on graduate-level physics and chemistry exams. The model introduces a new 'chain of thought' architecture that can decompose complex multi-step problems and verify its own reasoning, potentially transforming research workflows in healthcare and materials science.

tecnologia.titulo: Meta launches neural interface wristband with 99% accuracy for typing
tecnologia.resumen: Meta's Reality Labs division launched its neural interface wristband, which uses electromyography to translate muscle signals into text with 99% accuracy at speeds comparable to physical keyboard typing. The device, priced at $299, integrates with Meta's mixed reality headset and could redefine human-computer interaction for users with mobility impairments.

tecnologia.titulo: Microsoft Copilot+ PCs now auto-generate code from voice commands
tecnologia.resumen: Microsoft announced a major Copilot+ update enabling Copilot-powered Windows PCs to generate, test, and deploy code directly from natural language voice commands in English and Spanish. The feature integrates with Visual Studio Code and GitHub Copilot, allowing developers to describe application logic verbally and have working code generated in seconds.

tecnologia.titulo: Anthropic's Claude 4 scores highest ever on ARC-AGI benchmark
tecnologia.resumen: Anthropic's Claude 4 model achieved a score of 87.3% on the ARC-AGI benchmark, considered one of the hardest tests of general artificial intelligence, surpassing previous state-of-the-art by 12 percentage points. The breakthrough has reignited debate in the research community about the proximity of artificial general intelligence.

tecnologia.titulo: Starlink adds direct-to-cell voice calls in 47 new countries
tecnologia.resumen: SpaceX's Starlink direct-to-cell satellite service expanded to 47 additional countries, enabling standard smartphones to make voice calls via satellite without any special hardware. The expansion, which includes most of Latin America and Sub-Saharan Africa, could finally bring reliable mobile connectivity to the estimated 400 million people in rural areas without terrestrial coverage.

tecnologia.titulo: US-China chip war intensifies: TSMC restricts advanced node access for Chinese firms
tecnologia.resumen: TSMC has implemented new restrictions on Chinese customers seeking access to its 2nm and 3nm fabrication nodes, following updated US export regulations. The move accelerates China's drive for semiconductor self-sufficiency, with CXMT and SMIC announcing combined investments of over $50 billion in domestic advanced chip manufacturing over the next three years.
